criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: All interactive controls are keyboard accessible with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, or Space.
criteria.reasoning: Native buttons and card role-buttons are focusable; modal tabbing stayed within the dialog. Evidence: controls-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Modal dialogs trap focus and return it to the originating control on close.
criteria.reasoning: The create-task dialog trapped Tab focus and closing it returned focus to the originating Create task button. Evidence: modal-form-shared.png

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text
criteria.description: All images and icons have descriptive alt text.
criteria.reasoning: No img elements were present; SVG icons were marked aria-hidden and paired with descriptive visible labels where needed. Evidence: controls-shared.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Errors and notifications are conveyed both visually and via ARIA live regions.
criteria.reasoning: Validation feedback appeared visually as an alert with aria-live, and a Notifications region also used aria-live="polite". Evidence: modal-form-shared.png

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: All form elements use explicit label elements.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The visible form included an unlabeled native select element with no associated label and no aria-label, despite the custom combobox being labeled. Evidence: modal-form-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Headings follow a logical order with no skips, such as H1 to H2 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: The observed heading structure proceeds from H1 Repository intake to H2 repository headings without skips. Evidence: controls-shared.png

criteria.name: landmark_navigation_is_present
criteria.description: Skip-to-content or landmark navigation is present.
criteria.reasoning: The app exposes aside, nav, header, main, and a labeled Notifications region. Evidence: controls-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: All text and controls have sufficient color contrast.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The visible onboarding Skip control rendered with approximately 2.43:1 contrast against its dark background, below sufficient contrast. Evidence: a11y-initial-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Semantic HTML roles such as nav, main, and button are used.
criteria.reasoning: Observed semantic main/nav/aside/header elements, native buttons, and labeled ARIA roles for cards, dialogs, and notifications. Evidence: controls-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: Animations respect the prefers-reduced-motion setting.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched true and no visible elements had nonzero animation or transition durations. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_round_trip
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: configure several independent facets (e.g. active view, a membership change, a sort order, a created item), then reload. In persistence genres, all facets survive the reload; in in-memory genres, all facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline — never a mix.
criteria.reasoning: Configured sorting, created run #247, opened its detail, applied a trial filter, and expanded a log; reload returned to the seeded overview with default view, baseline counts, and no #247. Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: sort_reversal_proves_live_data
criteria.description: Sort-reversal proof: sort a collection ascending, note the order, then sort descending; the order reverses correctly relative to ascending, proving sort is derived from live data rather than two hardcoded orders.
criteria.reasoning: The live PR order changed from #247…#165 in Newest first to #165…#247 in Oldest first. Evidence: sort_reversal_proves_live_data-shared.png

criteria.name: derived_view_responds_to_input
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change an input (timeframe, filter, category) and confirm the derived view (chart, total, list) changes meaningfully rather than redrawing identically regardless of input.
criteria.reasoning: Searching different PR inputs changed the derived register: #247 produced a matching row, while another input produced an empty-state view. Evidence: derived_view_responds_to_input-shared.png

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_without_reload
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: make a change in one view and confirm the same datum updates in a second, structurally different view (list ↔ detail, board ↔ stats) without reload.
criteria.reasoning: After creating run #247, its pipeline row opened directly to a detail view showing the same PR number, title, issue, and accepted state without reload. Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png

criteria.name: count_delta_is_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure a count immediately before and immediately after the action that changes it; the delta matches exactly with no off-by-one or lagging counter.
criteria.reasoning: The quartz-orm summary changed immediately from 3 tasks produced to 4 after creating exactly one run (#247). Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png

criteria.name: different_inputs_change_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs and confirm the two outcomes differ in the ways the inputs dictate.
criteria.reasoning: The same search workflow with #247 returned a matching PR, while #184 returned no matches in the exercised state. Evidence: derived_view_responds_to_input-shared.png

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_preserve_state
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: interleave two flows (e.g. start creating item A, switch views, finish creating item B, return) and confirm neither flow corrupted the other's state.
criteria.reasoning: Entered PR #250 in the create dialog, switched to Analytics, returned to the pipeline, and the draft remained intact; submission completed as PR #250 with a matching detail view. Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: drive a collection to empty, then repopulate; derived surfaces (totals, charts, badges) track through both transitions.
criteria.reasoning: Empty register changed the derived totals to 0 and displayed the empty state; Restore seed register repopulated the rows and restored 9 processed, 3 tasks, and 33% yield. Evidence: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_resets_factory_baseline
criteria.description: After starting a create-task run, applying a trial-verdict filter, and expanding a log excerpt, a page reload coherently returns the app to its seeded baseline — seeded repositories, pull requests, trials, and timeline with the default view active, the created run's row gone, and no filters or expansions surviving — never a mix of kept and reset facets
criteria.reasoning: After creating runs and applying/expanding detail state, reload returned to the seeded overview: 4 repositories, 33 PRs, 18 accepted tasks, default view, and no created run or filters. Evidence: reload_resets_factory_baseline-shared.png

criteria.name: seeded_repositories_complete
criteria.description: On load, the Repositories view shows at least 4 fictional repositories, each with a name, primary language, pull-requests-processed count, tasks-produced count, and a yield percentage that equals tasks produced divided by pull requests processed (rounded to a whole percent)
criteria.reasoning: Four repositories are visible with language, processed count, produced tasks, and matching whole-percent yields. Evidence: seeded_repositories_complete-shared.png

criteria.name: repo_opens_pipeline_view
criteria.description: Selecting a repository swaps the main canvas to that repository's pipeline view without a full page reload, showing at least 8 seeded pull-request rows, each with a pull-request number, title, source-file count, and a linked-issue chip or a visible no-linked-issue marker; a visible control returns to the Repositories view
criteria.reasoning: Selecting quartz-orm opened its pipeline in-app with 9 PR rows and an All repositories return control. Evidence: repo_opens_pipeline_view-shared.png

criteria.name: stage_strip_five_stages
criteria.description: Every pull-request row renders a stage strip of exactly 5 named stages in the fixed order Fetch, Evaluate, Skeleton, Generate, Validate, and each stage shows exactly one of the statuses pending, running, complete, failed, or skipped with visually distinct treatments plus a readable label or tooltip
criteria.reasoning: Rows visibly show exactly Fetch, Evaluate, Skeleton, Generate, Validate with readable statuses including complete, skipped, running, and pending. Evidence: repo_opens_pipeline_view-shared.png

criteria.name: rejection_reason_taxonomy
criteria.description: Rejected pull requests show a rejection reason chip whose text is one of exactly five values — docs-only, formatting-only, too-few-files, too-many-files, no-linked-issue — and no rejection reason text outside that taxonomy appears anywhere in the app
criteria.reasoning: Rejected rows show only taxonomy reasons such as docs-only, formatting-only, too-few-files, and no-linked-issue; no out-of-taxonomy rejection reason was observed. Evidence: repo_opens_pipeline_view-shared.png

criteria.name: paired_check_cards
criteria.description: A task detail for a pull request that reached Validate shows two paired check cards side by side — a baseline check described as tests must fail on the reproduced bug and a reference check described as tests must pass with the fix applied — each with its own status, an attempt count, and a log excerpt that is collapsed by default and expands and collapses on activation
criteria.reasoning: Accepted task detail shows paired Baseline and Reference check cards with descriptions, passing statuses, Attempt 1, and collapsed Log excerpt controls; activation expanded and re-collapsed a log. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: accepted_badge_derives_from_checks
criteria.description: A task shows an accepted badge only when both the baseline check and the reference check show passing status; a seeded or created task with either check failing or pending shows no accepted badge, and when a created run's checks both turn green the badge appears without a reload
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Accepted badges were observed with both checks passing, including the created run, but the required failing or pending negative branch was not directly verified. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: trial_panel_distribution_bar
criteria.description: An accepted task's detail shows a trial analysis panel with at least 6 seeded trials, each carrying exactly one verdict from good-success, bad-success, good-failure, bad-failure, or infrastructure-error, plus a distribution bar whose segment widths are proportional to the verdict counts
criteria.reasoning: Accepted task detail shows 7 trials across the required verdict values and a proportional verdict distribution control. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: verdict_filter_round_trip
criteria.description: Activating a verdict in the distribution bar or its legend narrows the trial list to only trials with that verdict, and a clear control restores the full trial list exactly
criteria.reasoning: Activating bad-success narrowed the list to trial-02 only, and Clear filter restored all 7 trials. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: needs_review_banner_on_bad_success
criteria.description: A needs-review banner is visible on the detail of an accepted task that has at least one bad-success trial, and no such banner appears on an accepted task with zero bad-success trials
criteria.reasoning: Accepted task with one bad-success trial visibly shows Needs review. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: create_form_validation_rules
criteria.description: Submitting the create-task form with an empty or non-numeric pull-request number shows an inline message naming the pull-request number field and starts no run; entering a minimum source-file bound greater than the maximum shows a cross-field message naming both bounds, and the submit control stays disabled while the form is invalid
criteria.reasoning: Invalid nonnumeric PR input and min-greater-than-max bounds produced named inline errors while Start pipeline run remained disabled. Evidence: create_form_validation_rules-shared.png

criteria.name: created_run_stages_advance
criteria.description: Submitting a valid create-task form adds a new row to the chosen repository's pipeline view whose stage strip advances visibly through Fetch, Evaluate, Skeleton, Generate, and Validate, each stage moving from pending to running to complete over a perceivable interval rather than snapping instantly to a finished state
criteria.reasoning: Created PR #247 was observed mid-run with Skeleton running and later completed through all five stages, demonstrating interval-based advancement. Evidence: timeline_appends_live-shared.png

criteria.name: retry_resumes_from_failed_stage
criteria.description: During a created run, a failing stage shows a visible attempt count that increments on retry, and when the retry succeeds the run resumes from that stage while every earlier completed stage keeps its completed status and timestamp unchanged (no restart from the first stage)
criteria.reasoning: Created run visibly recorded Generate failed attempt 1, retry scheduled attempt 2, then completed with Generate a2 while earlier stage timestamps/statuses remained complete. Evidence: timeline_appends_live-shared.png

criteria.name: timeline_appends_live
criteria.description: The factory event timeline shows ordered, timestamped entries for stage transitions, gains entries live while a created run advances without a reload, supports filtering by event status, and restores the full list when the filter is cleared
criteria.reasoning: Timeline shows ordered timestamped transitions, including the created run's failure, retry, and acceptance; status filtering worked and Clear filter restored the full list. Evidence: timeline_appends_live-shared.png

criteria.name: charts_update_on_completion
criteria.description: The analytics area renders a tasks-per-week chart, a language distribution chart, and a difficulty histogram with hover tooltips showing underlying values, and after a created run completes as accepted, the tasks-per-week and language distribution charts change to include the new task without a reload
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Tasks-per-week, language distribution, and difficulty charts plus a hover tooltip were observed, but a direct before/after chart comparison was not captured. Evidence: charts_update_on_completion-shared.png

criteria.name: manifest_copy_confirmation
criteria.description: An accepted task's detail renders a task manifest in a monospaced code block with a format label and a copy control; activating copy places the exact manifest text on the clipboard and shows visible confirmation (icon swap or toast)
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Manifest code block and Copy control were present, but clipboard readback was denied and no visible confirmation was directly observed. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: create_task_request_body_field_contract
criteria.description: Create-task validates the request-body field contract: repository from the seeded select, pullRequestNumber a positive 1–6 digit integer string, minFiles and maxFiles integers 1–500 with minFiles ≤ maxFiles; violations keep submit disabled with named errors and start no run
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Required and cross-field validation was verified, but all specified range violations, including bounds outside 1–500, were not exercised. Evidence: create_form_validation_rules-shared.png

criteria.name: task_manifest_download_field_contract
criteria.description: Download task-manifest.json and Copy for an accepted task emit JSON with required schemaVersion 1, id, repository, pullRequestNumber, minFiles, maxFiles, checks.skeleton and checks.validate true, stages array of five named stages with status and attemptCount, and generatedAt ending in Z — matching the session-created task
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The visible manifest contains the required schema fields and five stages, but Download and exact emitted JSON were not directly verified. Evidence: paired_check_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: double_submit_single_run
criteria.description: Rapidly double-activating the create-task form's submit control starts exactly one run: exactly one new row appears in the pipeline view
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Rapid double activation of the valid submit control was not exercised. Evidence: create_form_validation_rules-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_on_no_match
criteria.description: When a verdict filter or timeline filter matches nothing, the filtered region shows an empty state message with a control that clears the filter, instead of a blank or broken panel
criteria.reasoning: Filtering the timeline to skipped showed a dedicated No events match empty state with a Clear filter control. Evidence: timeline_appends_live-shared.png

criteria.name: seed_covers_three_pipeline_situations
criteria.description: In at least one repository the seeded pull requests include at least one fully complete and accepted PR, at least one rejected PR showing a taxonomy reason chip, and at least one mid-pipeline PR whose stage strip still shows a pending or running stage
criteria.reasoning: The seeded quartz-orm pipeline visibly includes accepted complete rows, rejected taxonomy-chip rows, and a mid-pipeline row with running/pending stages. Evidence: repo_opens_pipeline_view-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow the design system's scale, with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles.
criteria.reasoning: Consistent card, button, sidebar, and grid spacing is visible across the desktop layout. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the spec for all headings and body copy.
criteria.reasoning: Inter is used consistently, with visibly distinct heading, label, and body sizes/weights. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference
criteria.description: Layout matches reference screenshots to within a small tolerance at all specified breakpoints; fidelity tasks achieve near pixel-parity.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop composition shows a stable sidebar, header actions, metric strip, and two-column repository cards. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Transitions or animations are applied to the state changes the spec calls out, such as add, delete, filter, or view switch.
criteria.reasoning: Controls expose short CSS transitions, and the theme state changes through the real Dark-theme interaction. Evidence: theme-state-shared.png

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference
criteria.description: Responsive behavior matches reference patterns.
criteria.reasoning: At 390px the layout switches to a compact mobile header and vertically stacked content without horizontal overflow. Evidence: responsive-shared.png

criteria.name: control_styling_matches_spec
criteria.description: Button and form radii, padding, and shadows conform to the spec.
criteria.reasoning: Buttons consistently use rounded corners, compact padding, and restrained shadows; hover styling changes the primary button visibly. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_has_clear_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography has a clear hierarchy that distinguishes section and body text.
criteria.reasoning: Large page titles, uppercase section labels, card titles, and supporting copy form a clear hierarchy. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Component states (default, hover, active, disabled) match the spec where specified.
criteria.reasoning: Selected navigation/theme controls and hover feedback are visibly differentiated from resting controls. Evidence: theme-state-shared.png

criteria.name: surface_treatments_match_spec
criteria.description: Color, depth, and border treatments are precise per the design.
criteria.reasoning: The app uses distinct dark navigation surfaces, light content surfaces, bordered cards, and subtle depth treatments. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_spec
criteria.description: Microinteraction feedback for hover and press follows the spec in duration and motion.
criteria.reasoning: Real hover observation changed background, shadow, and transform, with a short transition duration. Evidence: desktop-final-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_state_is_present
criteria.description: Empty state UI is present and renders when a collection is empty.
criteria.reasoning: Filtered to a verdict with zero trials and observed “No trials match” with guidance to clear the filter. Evidence: empty_state_is_present-shared.png

criteria.name: forms_validate_inline
criteria.description: Inline validation appears on all forms before submit.
criteria.reasoning: The create-task form showed inline required and invalid-value messages before submission. Evidence: forms_validate_inline-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_are_actionable
criteria.description: Error messages are clear and actionable rather than only saying 'Invalid'.
criteria.reasoning: Invalid PR input produced the actionable message “Pull-request number must be a positive integer of 1–6 digits.” Evidence: forms_validate_inline-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_show_confirmation
criteria.description: Success or confirmation feedback appears after actions.
criteria.reasoning: After starting a run, notifications visibly reported task acceptance and run completion. Evidence: actions_show_confirmation-shared.png

criteria.name: async_work_shows_loading_state
criteria.description: Loading spinners or indicators are shown for async or simulated-async work.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The simulated pipeline run completed, but no loading spinner, progress indicator, or aria-busy state was observed during the interaction. Evidence: actions_show_confirmation-shared.png

criteria.name: destructive_actions_support_undo_or_cancel
criteria.description: Undo or cancel options are available where destructive actions exist.
criteria.reasoning: No destructive collection actions such as delete, reset, or clear-data were observed; the available filter-clearing control is reversible.

criteria.name: non_obvious_controls_have_help
criteria.description: Help or tooltips are present for non-obvious controls.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Icon-only controls had accessible labels, but no visible help text or tooltip was observed for non-obvious controls. Evidence: empty_state_is_present-shared.png

criteria.name: controls_use_semantic_tags
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, and labels use semantic tags.
criteria.reasoning: Observed native button, input, select/combobox, label, dialog, table, and alert elements in the live UI. Evidence: forms_validate_inline-shared.png

criteria.name: modal_supports_close_paths
criteria.description: Modal close controls and background clicks both close the overlay.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The create-task dialog’s explicit close control worked, but clicking the background outside the dialog left the dialog open. Evidence: modal_supports_close_paths-shared.png

criteria.name: long_flows_show_progress
criteria.description: Long task flows provide progress or feedback steps.
criteria.reasoning: The completed pipeline visibly presented five ordered factory stages with statuses and attempt counts. Evidence: actions_show_confirmation-shared.png

criteria.name: long_pr_title_truncates
criteria.description: A pull-request title longer than 60 characters renders truncated with an ellipsis in its pipeline row and appears in full in the task or PR detail
criteria.reasoning: A title over 60 characters had overflow hidden, ellipsis, and a smaller client width than scroll width in the pipeline row; the same full title appeared in the PR detail heading. Evidence: long_pr_title_truncates-shared.png; long_pr_title_truncates-detail.png

criteria.name: create_cancel_changes_nothing
criteria.description: Cancel or close on the create-task form leaves all collections unchanged: the pipeline row count, repository counts, and timeline are identical before and after
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Cancel left the pipeline row count and repository metrics unchanged, but the observed timeline changed from 11 to 12 events before/after the cancel workflow, so the required identical state was not fully verified. Evidence: create_cancel_changes_nothing-shared.png

criteria.name: delightful_microinteractions
criteria.description: The app has unique, delightful microinteractions.
criteria.reasoning: Observed animated factory-stage status pills and immediate voice-fill toast feedback. Evidence: delightful-microinteractions-shared.png

criteria.name: advanced_motion_mechanics
criteria.description: The app has advanced animation or transition mechanics such as parallax or scroll storytelling.
criteria.reasoning: Observed scroll-story behavior; after PageDown, the story element had a non-zero transform (matrix translateY -11.616px). Evidence: advanced-motion-mechanics-shared.png

criteria.name: guided_onboarding
criteria.description: The app has a narrative or guided onboarding flow.
criteria.reasoning: Completed the live 3-step onboarding flow from Welcome through Create a run to Read the signals/Analytics. Evidence: innovation-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: enhanced_interactive_graphics
criteria.description: Data visualizations or interactive graphics provide extra usability.
criteria.reasoning: Analytics visibly provides interactive line, donut, and histogram charts with hover guidance and task-value inspection. Evidence: enhanced-interactive-graphics-shared.png

criteria.name: alternative_input_support
criteria.description: The app supports voice, gesture, or another alternative input.
criteria.reasoning: Clicked Voice fill (demo); it filled the pull-request field with 999 and showed a Voice draft filled notification. Evidence: alternative-input-support-shared.png

criteria.name: preference_personalization
criteria.description: The app provides personalization features for user preferences.
criteria.reasoning: Observed live personalization controls for theme, density, and gesture shortcuts. Evidence: dynamic-theming-beyond-requirements-shared.png

criteria.name: polished_brand_narrative
criteria.description: The app presents polished storytelling or a branding narrative.
criteria.reasoning: The app presents a cohesive Forgebeam Factory narrative across Factory overview, Pipeline register, Factory intelligence, stages, and online status. Evidence: brand-narrative-shared.png

criteria.name: dynamic_theming_beyond_requirements
criteria.description: The app provides dynamic theming or a color mode beyond requirements.
criteria.reasoning: Clicked Dark and directly observed the interface switch to a dark visual theme with the Dark control selected. Evidence: dynamic-theming-beyond-requirements-shared.png

criteria.name: genre_appropriate_platform_features
criteria.description: The app uses web platform features such as PWA or offline support where the genre allows.
criteria.reasoning: Browser inspection found a manifest link at /manifest.json and an activated service worker scoped to the app origin; the UI also visibly identifies an offline-ready installable shell. Evidence: brand-narrative-shared.png

criteria.name: competition_level_innovation
criteria.description: Competition-level innovation is visible to users.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The app is polished and featureful, but no clearly competition-level breakthrough or unusually novel interaction was directly observed. Evidence: brand-narrative-shared.png

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The noteworthy enhancements observed are already covered by the other criteria: onboarding, motion, charts, alternative input, personalization, theming, and PWA support. Evidence: innovation-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: hover_feedback_required
criteria.description: Hovering with the real pointer shows visible feedback: buttons ease background and shadow with a slight press effect, table rows and nav items take a full-width hover wash, interactive chips respond, and form controls show focus rings
criteria.reasoning: Direct hover changed the navigation button background, inset shadow, and transform while retaining a transition. Evidence: hover_feedback_required-shared.png

criteria.name: create_dialog_enter_exit
criteria.description: Opening the create-task control with the real pointer brings the form dialog in with a short opacity and scale transition of roughly 200 to 300 milliseconds, and closing exits the same way rather than disappearing instantly
criteria.reasoning: Real-pointer opening and closing showed opacity/scale transitions over roughly 200–300ms. Evidence: create_dialog_enter_exit-shared.png

criteria.name: run_and_timeline_microinteractions
criteria.description: Driven through the real UI controls (never a state-shortcut tool call): submitting the create-task form animates the new run row into the pipeline table, new timeline entries animate in rather than popping, the currently running stage carries a visible animated indicator (pulse or spinner) that settles when the stage completes, and activating a verdict filter animates the distribution bar segments and trial rows to their new arrangement rather than snapping
criteria.reasoning: A real form submission produced an animated run row, a running stage spinner, timeline entries with eventIn animation, and verdict filtering reduced trials with fadeMove animation. Evidence: run_and_timeline_microinteractions-shared.png

criteria.name: log_disclosure_transition
criteria.description: Activating a log excerpt disclosure expands it with a smooth height transition and a chevron rotation, and activating it again collapses it the same way
criteria.reasoning: Real-pointer disclosure toggling showed chevron rotation with a 0.2s transform transition and expanded/collapsed content. Evidence: log_disclosure_transition-shared.png

criteria.name: toasts_slide_autodismiss
criteria.description: After starting a run, on run completion, and after copying the manifest, a feedback toast slides in, remains readable long enough to scan, and auto-dismisses with a fade
criteria.reasoning: Start and completion toasts appeared with toastLife animation, and the copy toast appeared then disappeared after its lifecycle. Evidence: toasts_slide_autodismiss-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion enabled, animations are removed or reduced to instant state changes while every flow — create, run progression, disclosures, filters — remains fully usable
criteria.reasoning: The reduced-motion browser matched prefers-reduced-motion, create remained usable, and computed transition/animation durations were all 0s after run progression. Evidence: reduced_motion_respected-shared.png

criteria.name: cold_start_is_under_two_seconds
criteria.description: Cold start to interactive is under 2 seconds on local render.
criteria.reasoning: DOM became interactive in about 212 ms after navigation; the page rendered normally. Evidence: perf-ready-shared.png

criteria.name: console_is_clean
criteria.description: Browser devtools show no errors or warnings.
criteria.reasoning: Playwright reported 0 errors and 0 warnings. Evidence: perf-ready-shared.png

criteria.name: transitions_respond_under_100ms
criteria.description: UI transitions respond in under 100ms.
criteria.reasoning: Observed UI transition durations were 70 ms, under the 100 ms threshold. Evidence: perf-theme-shared.png

criteria.name: async_work_has_loading_indicators
criteria.description: Loading indicators are shown for async or simulated delays.
criteria.reasoning: No async or simulated-delay workflow was exposed during testing, so no missing loading state was observed. Evidence: perf-ready-shared.png

criteria.name: large_collections_render_without_lag
criteria.description: Large collections render without perceived lag.
criteria.reasoning: The app exposed only a small four-repository collection, which rendered immediately without perceived lag. Evidence: perf-repositories-shared.png

criteria.name: state_changes_remain_interactive
criteria.description: The UI remains interactive during state changes.
criteria.reasoning: Repeated navigation state changes completed successfully and the app remained responsive throughout. Evidence: perf-rapid-shared.png

criteria.name: animations_maintain_smooth_frame_rate
criteria.description: Animations maintain a smooth 60fps; games maintain a stable frame rate during play.
criteria.reasoning: A 500 ms requestAnimationFrame sample produced 32 frames, with a maximum gap of 16.8 ms and no gaps over 25 ms. Evidence: perf-rapid-shared.png

criteria.name: rapid_input_does_not_freeze
criteria.description: The app does not hang or freeze even under rapid user input.
criteria.reasoning: Eight rapid navigation cycles completed in 1.465 seconds with the app still rendered and interactive. Evidence: perf-rapid-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_adapts_desktop_to_mobile
criteria.description: Layout adapts gracefully from 1440px desktop to 375px mobile.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop uses a sidebar and two-column cards; mobile switches to a compact header and single-column cards. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_are_large_enough
criteria.description: UI controls are tap targets at least 44px on mobile.
criteria.reasoning: Measured mobile controls are generally 44px high, including navigation, theme, create-task, card, and dialog buttons. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_resizes_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: Typography resizes for both mobile and desktop.
criteria.reasoning: The main heading changes from 36px on desktop to 26px on mobile. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: content_avoids_clipping_and_overflow
criteria.description: Content never clips or overflows the viewport.
criteria.reasoning: Mobile content remains within the viewport width and scrolls vertically without visible horizontal clipping. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: chrome_adapts_to_small_screens
criteria.description: Collapsible chrome such as sidebars and menus adapts for smaller screens.
criteria.reasoning: The desktop sidebar collapses behind an Open navigation button on mobile; tapping it opened the navigation drawer. Evidence: mobile-nav-shared.png

criteria.name: stacking_reflows_logically
criteria.description: Layout stacking order reflows logically at narrow widths.
criteria.reasoning: The desktop card grid reflows into a readable single-column sequence on mobile, with header, controls, summary, and content ordered logically. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_touch_gestures_work
criteria.description: Touch gestures such as swipe and tap work on mobile where present.
criteria.reasoning: A real mobile-sized tap on Open navigation successfully opened the navigation drawer. Evidence: mobile-nav-shared.png

criteria.name: small_screens_avoid_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: Small screens have no horizontal scrolling.
criteria.reasoning: At 375px viewport width, measured document scroll width was 360px, with no horizontal overflow. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: media_and_canvases_resize
criteria.description: Images, grids, and canvases size responsively to fit the layout.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered cards and interface imagery fit the mobile content width and remain responsive; no oversized canvas or media was present. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: fixed_controls_remain_accessible
criteria.description: Fixed controls remain accessible on all devices.
criteria.reasoning: Mobile header controls and the fixed guided-setup dialog remain within the viewport and expose usable tap targets; desktop fixed dialog is also visible. Evidence: desktop-shared.png, mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: All views derive from one shared state: a completed accepted run is reflected at once in the repository counts, the analytics charts, the event timeline, and the task detail without a reload, and no surface shows a value another surface already updated
criteria.reasoning: Completed run #247 visibly reached Accepted with all stages complete; repository, task detail, analytics, and timeline reflected the run without reload. Evidence: shared_state_coherence-complete.png, shared_state_coherence-analytics-ui.png, shared_state_coherence-timeline-ui.png

criteria.name: no_storage_reload_seeded
criteria.description: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after exercising the app (creating runs, filtering, expanding disclosures), and a page reload returns the app to its seeded state with the default view active
criteria.reasoning: localStorage and sessionStorage stayed empty, and reload returned to the repository view with seeded repositories and default navigation active. Evidence: no_storage_reload_seeded-shared.png

criteria.name: console_clean_during_session
criteria.description: No console errors, warnings, or unhandled promise rejections appear on load or during a full exercise of the app, including while simulated runs are advancing
criteria.reasoning: Console inspection after load, run progression, navigation, and rapid input reported zero errors and warnings. Evidence: cold_load_and_rapid_input-shared.png

criteria.name: keyboard_operability_focus
criteria.description: Every interactive control — nav items, repository entries, disclosures, filters, form fields, and the copy control — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab/Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), showing a visible focus indicator at each stop
criteria.reasoning: Keyboard tabbing reached navigation, controls, repository entries, and onboarding controls; each observed focus stop had a visible 2px green outline. Evidence: no_storage_reload_seeded-shared.png

criteria.name: dialog_focus_labels
criteria.description: The create-task dialog uses a dialog role, traps focus while open, closes on Escape returning focus to its trigger; form fields have visible labels, each validation message names and is associated with its field (or pair of bound fields), and stage statuses, verdicts, and rejection reasons are conveyed by text or accessible labels in addition to color
criteria.reasoning: Create-task dialog exposed a dialog role, visible field labels, required-field alert text, textual stage/verdict statuses, and Escape closed it while returning focus to the Create task trigger. Evidence: dialog_focus_labels-shared.png

criteria.name: cold_load_and_rapid_input
criteria.description: The app is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load, stays responsive during simulated runs and under rapid repeated input (fast view switches, filter toggling) with no hangs, and async-feeling surfaces show a deliberate loading affordance rather than a blank region
criteria.reasoning: Cold navigation completed in about 1.4 seconds, rapid repeated view switching completed in 452ms, and the app remained responsive with no console errors. Simulated runs showed deliberate advancing stage states rather than blank async regions. Evidence: cold_load_and_rapid_input-shared.png

criteria.name: create_task_advances_pipeline_and_metrics
criteria.description: Create-task flow: submit the create form with valid repository, pullRequestNumber, and file bounds; the new pipeline row's stage strip advances through Fetch→Evaluate→Skeleton→Generate→Validate including one visible failure-and-retry; on completion the repository's tasks-produced count increases by exactly one, yield recomputes, tasks-per-week includes the new task, and the event timeline contains that run's stage entries — without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: Accepted PR #247 run visibly completed Fetch→Evaluate→Skeleton→Generate→Validate; Generate showed attempt 2 after failure, and the detail showed accepted status, manifest, and review data. Evidence: create_task_advances_pipeline_and_metrics.png

criteria.name: invalid_create_task_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create flow: empty/non-numeric pull-request number or minFiles greater than maxFiles shows immediate inline validation naming the offending field(s), keeps submit disabled while invalid, and starts no run (pipeline row count unchanged).
criteria.reasoning: Invalid PR number and min/max bounds produced immediate field-specific inline alerts and kept Start pipeline run disabled. Evidence: invalid_create_task_inline_validation.png

criteria.name: retry_resume_preserves_completed_stages
criteria.description: Retry/resume flow: during a created run, the failing stage's attempt count increments, then the run resumes from that stage while every earlier stage keeps its completed status and timestamp unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: The completed run visibly showed Generate attempt 2 while Fetch, Evaluate, and Skeleton remained complete with their original timestamps. Evidence: create_task_advances_pipeline_and_metrics.png

criteria.name: verdict_filter_and_needs_review
criteria.description: Trial filter flow: on an accepted task, activate the bad-success verdict; the trial list shows only bad-success trials and the needs-review banner is visible; clearing the filter restores the full trial list.
criteria.reasoning: Bad-success filtering reduced the trial list to the bad-success trial while Needs review remained visible; clearing restored the full list. Evidence: verdict_filter_and_needs_review.png

criteria.name: repos_pipeline_detail_navigation_retains_state
criteria.description: Navigation: move from Repositories to a repository pipeline to a task detail and back; previously applied filters and expanded log excerpts in the detail keep their state while the app stays open.
criteria.reasoning: After navigating repositories→pipeline→detail, the bad-success filter and expanded Baseline log excerpt were still visibly retained. Evidence: repos_pipeline_detail_navigation_retains_state.png

criteria.name: empty_or_filtered_pipeline_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: when a filter or selection matches no pull requests/trials, the list region shows a designed empty state explaining what belongs there rather than a blank broken area.
criteria.reasoning: A zero-result status filter displayed a designed “No events match” state explaining that the underlying record was unchanged. Evidence: timeline_status_filter_coherence.png

criteria.name: timeline_status_filter_coherence
criteria.description: Timeline filter: filtering the factory event timeline by event status narrows visible entries; clearing the filter restores the full timeline without removing underlying records.
criteria.reasoning: Filtering timeline events by failed showed 2 of 12 failed entries; the prior unfiltered view showed all 12, and clearing restored the timeline. Evidence: timeline_status_filter_coherence.png

criteria.name: analytics_and_timeline_chrome_continuity
criteria.description: Chrome: switching among Repositories, pipeline, Timeline, and Analytics keeps the shared session runs; charts remain interactive (hover tooltips) without navigating away from the factory shell.
criteria.reasoning: Repositories, pipeline/detail, Timeline, and Analytics were reachable within the shared shell; Analytics rendered three chart applications and hovering a mark displayed a tooltip. Evidence: create_task_advances_pipeline_and_metrics.png

criteria.name: create_and_manifest_overlays
criteria.description: Overlays: Create task form and task-manifest Copy/Download surfaces open, complete their flows, and dismiss without trapping navigation.
criteria.reasoning: The Create benchmark task dialog opened and was dismissed; accepted-task detail exposed Copy and Download manifest controls, and both actions completed without trapping navigation. Evidence: create_and_manifest_overlays.png

criteria.name: manifest_export_and_failed_create_recover
criteria.description: Recovery: after a rejected create, the form can be corrected and submitted without reload; after an accepted run, task-manifest Copy/Download emit matching text including pullRequestNumber and file bounds.
criteria.reasoning: A rejected form state was corrected and submitted without reload; accepted detail showed PR/file bounds in the manifest, Copy produced a clipboard notification, and Download emitted a completed download event. Evidence: manifest_export_after_accepted_create.png

criteria.name: manifest_export_after_accepted_create
criteria.description: Manifest export flow: complete a create-task run to accepted, open the task detail, confirm the monospaced manifest includes that pullRequestNumber and file bounds; Copy and Download emit the same text; a second accepted create increases any factory-wide export length by one.
criteria.reasoning: Two accepted creates completed (#247 and #248); the second task detail visibly showed accepted status and a monospaced manifest containing pullRequestNumber 248, minFiles 3, and maxFiles 9, with Copy and Download controls. Evidence: manifest_export_after_accepted_create.png

criteria.name: dashboard_shell_composition
criteria.description: The app presents an operations-dashboard shell: a fixed left navigation listing Repositories, Timeline, and Analytics destinations plus a Create task control, and a main canvas that changes per destination, with the pipeline view rendered as a dense table register containing the inline stage strip per row
criteria.reasoning: Desktop view visibly shows a fixed left navigation with Repositories, Timeline, Analytics, and Create task; the main canvas presents repository cards and a dense pipeline table with inline stage strips. Evidence: dashboard_shell_composition-shared.png

criteria.name: stage_status_color_system
criteria.description: Stage statuses use one consistent color system everywhere they appear — pending in a muted neutral, running in an accented treatment, complete in green, failed in red, skipped in a dimmed or hatched treatment — always paired with a readable label or tooltip rather than color alone
criteria.reasoning: Pipeline stages visibly use green complete, blue running, muted pending, and hatched gray skipped treatments, each paired with readable labels. Evidence: stage_status_color_system-shared.png

criteria.name: verdict_hue_consistency
criteria.description: The five trial verdicts use five distinct hues, and the distribution bar segments, verdict chips, and legend swatches use the same hue for the same verdict
criteria.reasoning: Trial analysis visibly shows five distinct hues consistently reused across the distribution bar, verdict chips, legend swatches, and trial verdict labels. Evidence: verdict_hue_consistency-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography shows a clear hierarchy: page titles visibly larger than section headings, which are larger than table body and chip text, applied consistently across views; numeric rollups (counts, yield percentages) render in an aligned, tabular style
criteria.reasoning: The live views show clear title, section-heading, body, and chip hierarchy; dashboard rollups and yield figures are visually aligned and tabular. Evidence: dashboard_shell_composition-shared.png

criteria.name: monospace_code_surfaces
criteria.description: The task manifest block and expanded log excerpts render on a visually distinct monospaced surface, while check cards, trial panels, and chart cards use cards with hairline borders and subtle shadows and one consistent icon set is used across nav, chips, and controls
criteria.reasoning: The task manifest and expanded log excerpt visibly render on dark monospaced surfaces; check cards, trial panels, and controls use bordered cards with subtle shadows and consistent line icons. Evidence: monospace_code_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: responsive_reflow_clean
criteria.description: At 768 pixels and below the left navigation collapses behind a menu control that opens an overlay drawer, and at 375 pixel width no content clips or overflows the viewport, no page-level horizontal scrolling appears, and dense tables and stage strips scroll within their own containers
criteria.reasoning: At 768px an Open navigation control opens an overlay drawer; at 375px content reflows without page-level overflow, while measurements show the table and stage strip use internal horizontal scrolling containers. Evidence: responsive_reflow_clean-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).
criteria.reasoning: Headings consistently use sentence case, including “Repository intake,” “Validation checks,” and “Task manifest.” Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs ('Add expense', 'Start session'), not generic ('Submit', 'OK') when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Actions use clear labels such as “Create task,” “Start pipeline run,” “Download task-manifest.json,” and “Dismiss notification.” Evidence: success-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix ('Amount must be a positive number'), not just a rejection ('Invalid').
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The visible validation error says only “Pull-request number is required”; it identifies the problem but does not explicitly state the corrective action. Evidence: error-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it.
criteria.reasoning: No empty-state panels were rendered in the observed views, so there was no empty-state copy to evaluate. Evidence: timeline-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Observed body copy is clear, grammatical, and free of apparent spelling errors. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (not 'task' here and 'todo' there).
criteria.reasoning: The app consistently uses repository, pull request, task, pipeline, stage, and trial terminology across views. Evidence: timeline-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units, formatting is consistent (same date format, same decimal precision, units labeled).
criteria.reasoning: Counts and percentages use consistent formatting, durations use m ss, and timestamps consistently include UTC. Evidence: timeline-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened ('Entry saved'), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: The confirmation explicitly states “Run started for quartz-orm #247,” identifying what happened and which item it affected. Evidence: success-shared.png
